item.text:
Active bounded unit: fix the executed-implementer handoff advancement in persisted run-graph status, plus one focused regression test for that exact downstream-packet-ready case. Why this unit: the packet explicitly scopes the bug to `apply_first_handoff_execution_to_run_graph_status` and the minimal regression surface; posture: sequential, because the code change and test target the same handoff transition path.

Plan:
1. Inspect the two scoped files and locate the advancement logic plus existing tests around first-handoff execution.
2. Patch the run-graph status transition so an executed implementer receipt with `downstream_dispatch_ready=true` persists the next lawful downstream handoff state instead of stranding `active_node=implementer`.
3. Add one regression test for the exact executed-implementer plus downstream-packet-ready case, then run the narrowest relevant test target and report evidence or blocker.

I’m in the scoped transition logic now. Next step is to isolate `apply_first_handoff_execution_to_run_graph_status` and the closest test coverage, then patch only that advancement path and the one regression case.
